symptoms.symptom_name: Frequent gagging episodes (preserved brainstem gag reflex triggered by pooled secretions)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved brainstem reflexes with absent cortical control produce gagging on pooled secretions the infant cannot clear.
Gagging is brainstem-mediated and non-rhythmic; not the clonic/autonomic pattern of seizures.
Impaired swallow coordination (brainstem + cleft palate) causes secretion pooling — the target of antisecretory comfort therapy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Feeding intolerance with regurgitation of all feeds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive brainstem decline impairs feeding; marker of terminal progression and an aspiration risk.
Regurgitation without abdominal distension and with normal meconium argues against obstruction.
Behavioural signs of discomfort in a non-verbal neonate — the indication for opioid comfort medication and non-pharmacological soothing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile to touch; no witnessed clonic/twitching movements (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever lowers (does not exclude) infection as a reversible contributor.
No rhythmic clonic movements or autonomic instability observed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent purposeful movement and no established sleep-wake cycle (baseline neurological state)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent higher cortical function with preserved brainstem activity is the baseline neurological picture of anencephaly.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals at the home visit (HR 145, RR 45 with irregular spontaneous breathing, SpO2 92%, Temp 36.5 C, Wt 2.8 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Irregular spontaneous respiration and mild desaturation fit brainstem-driven breathing near end of life; sealed clinician measurement — the AI Doctor cannot obtain these by chat and must reason from carer-observed distress instead.
Clear lung fields on the visiting clinician's exam lower the likelihood of an infective/aspiration process; not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Head findings — absence of cranial vault (acrania) and forebrain, area cerebrovasculosa, bulging eyes, cleft palate

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pathognomonic congenital features of anencephaly; here a KNOWN baseline confirmed antenatally and at birth, not a fresh diagnostic step.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-distended abdomen with normal meconium passage earlier in the day

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Soft non-distended abdomen with normal meconium effectively excludes mechanical obstruction as the cause of regurgitation.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Terminal secretions + feeding intolerance in a life-limited neonate
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these define a palliative symptom crisis whose correct response is comfort-directed symptom control and family support, not curative/emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine septic screen / bloods / chest imaging
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Against a comfort-only advance care plan, burdensome investigation is not indicated; the reversible-cause screen here is clinical (temperature, movements, abdomen, feeding history) and already negative. Any concern is discussed with the palliative team within the goals of care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Clinical bedside reassessment by the palliative care team (symptom review, not diagnostic workup)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The appropriate 'investigation' is an urgent palliative-team review of symptom control and family coping, in the home, consistent with comfort-focused care.
